name: CompactButton
description: Small square icon-only button (e.g. dismiss/close). 4 styles (stroke/ghost/modifiable/white), 2 sizes (24px/20px), rounded-square or full-circle radius, 4 states (default/hover/active/disabled).
sourceNote: First built from an AI Handoff visual pack while the Figma MCP tool-call limit was hit (only Default/Hover were captured there); then verified and corrected directly against the Figma MCP (Align UI Design System 2.0) once the user upgraded their plan. The MCP trace corrected several guesses: radius-off is radius-6 (not radius-8), Active uses a solid --color-bg-surface (#262626) background with a white icon (not a dark-gray one), and Modifiable's hover/active use a translucent white overlay (--alpha-white-alpha-16, plus a white border on active) rather than opacity dimming.

style.description: stroke = white bordered + shadow; ghost = transparent; modifiable = inherits currentColor (for colored parents like Alert/Banner); white = white surface + shadow, no border

state.description: Forces a visual state for design QA (e.g. side-by-side galleries). Leave unset in real usage — native :hover/:active/:focus-visible drive Default/Hover/Active, and the `disabled` prop drives Disabled. `state="disabled"` also sets the real disabled attribute so the visual and the semantics never diverge.
